title: GLM-5.2 CPU serve on a high-RAM no-GPU box: single-stream prefill/decode tok/s for the full ~433GB UD-Q4_K_M. MEMORY: the fak-native all-resident path needs >= ~1.1x model-size of FREE RAM (it loaded ~458GB resident and WEDGED a 1TB host where ~512GB was hugepage-locked, 2026-06-27 / #974); run llama.cpp (mmap, memory-safe) for the baseline, and the fak-native path ONLY when free RAM clears the model+KV.
run: BASELINE (memory-safe, mmap): /path/to/llama-bench -m $FAK_GGUF -ngl 0 -t 128 -p 256 -n 64 -r 2   ||   FAK-NATIVE (only if free RAM > ~1.1x GGUF size + KV; else it OOM-wedges the host, #974): FAK_Q4K=1 FAK_GGUF_LOAD_WORKERS=64 fak serve --gguf $FAK_GGUF --context-budget-tokens 8192 --addr 127.0.0.1:8080  then POST /v1/chat/completions
acceptance: a recorded single-stream prefill+decode tok/s for GLM-5.2 UD-Q4_K_M on a CPU-only host for llama.cpp (mmap baseline) and, where RAM allows, fak-native, in an experiments/benchmark/runs artifact; the fak-native resident-Q4_K load wall-clock + GB/s where it fits

title: Qwen3.6-27B Q4_K_M single-stream decode tok/s in fak's OWN Apple-Silicon Metal kernel (engine=inkernel, --metal), NOT coder14b/llama.cpp. The decode-length curve (16/32/64/128 tok) traces the prefill-amortization asymptote. The 36GB Mac needs --context-budget-tokens to bound KV (the full native window is 192GiB -> FitTooBig).
run: On the Mac metal node (a -tags fakmetal fak build): FAK_Q4K=1 FAK_GATEWAY_KEY=$(cat ~/.fak-gateway-key) fak serve --metal --gguf <mac>/.cache/fak-models/gguf/Qwen3.6-27B.q4_k_m.gguf --model qwen3.6-27b --addr $FAK_MAC_GATEWAY --require-key-env FAK_GATEWAY_KEY --context-budget-tokens 8192  then curl /v1/chat/completions max_tokens={16,32,64,128} and record completion_tokens/time_total. WARM the serve first (discard the cold 16-tok point). Real mac host/user resolve from the gitignored fak-mac.local.ps1 (see docs/fak/scrubbing-real-values.md).
acceptance: a recorded warm single-stream decode tok/s curve for Qwen3.6-27B Q4_K_M on the Mac Metal node, labelled WITNESSED (fak own kernel); the cold first-request point flagged separately
